authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Quail Lake not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Quail Lake not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50(b)(152) — Tahoe Lake and tributaries (the tributary closure applies to streams 'upstream to the first lake', not to the lakes/reservoirs themselves)
sources.quotedText: (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado Cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout (B) Tahoe Lake within 300 feet of the mouth of its tributaries. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: Public access to Quail Lake looks likely — Sierra Sun
sources.quotedText: The public may soon have the opportunity to visit a pristine 11-acre fishing lake on the West Shore that Homewood Mountain Resort has privately operated since 1992. ... The U.S. Forest Service is working to acquire 265 acres in the southern portion of the ski area, including Quail Lake and Quail Creek.

parameters.note: Quail Lake is an ~11-acre dammed reservoir on Quail Creek on the West Shore above Homewood, Placer Co. It was operated as a private fishing lake by Homewood Mountain Resort (since 1992) and sits on land the U.S. Forest Service moved to acquire for the Lake Tahoe Basin Management Unit. For sport-fishing take rules it is not listed by name in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so the general statewide lake regulation §5.85(a)(1) applies (open all year, five-trout daily / ten-trout possession). Note: the Lake Tahoe tributary closure §7.50(b)(152)(A) applies to tributary streams 'upstream to the first lake' and does not extend to the reservoir itself. Any historical trout stocking here was a private operation; Quail Lake is not listed in CDFW's 2026 provisional stocking plan, so no stocking or species is asserted. Access may be governed by the landowner/USFS separately — verify on the ground.

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide lake regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water, and the Lake Tahoe tributary closure applies only to streams upstream to the first lake, not to the reservoir)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. Quail Lake is wholly within California (Placer Co.); no Nevada reciprocity applies.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
